title: BMBL Q1 Deep Dive: Member Base Reset and Tech Overhaul Shape Outlook
text: Bumble Inc. Reports Q1 2026 Earnings with revenue down 14.1% year-over-year and EPS beat of $0.34 versus consensus of $0.26. The stock dropped 19% in the week since its latest quarterly results, despite the earnings beat. Operating profit was up 46.2% year-over-year, and net income attributable to common shareholders was up 236.3% year-over-year. Following the recent earnings report, the consensus from 16 analysts covering Bumble is for revenues of US$846.6m in 2026, which implies an uneasy 9.1% decline in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Bumble is also expected to turn profitable, with statutory earnings of US$1.01 per share. Next quarter's revenue guidance of $209 million was less impressive, coming in 3.1% below analysts' estimates. The analysts seem to have become a little more negative on the business after the latest results, given the small dip in their earnings per share numbers for next year. The company is undergoing member base reset initiatives and tech overhaul to position for sustainable long-term growth despite near-term revenue challenges.
